title: NOC 72203 – Electrical power line and cable workers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 72203 professionals, commonly known as Powerline Technicians or Lineworkers, are the essential frontline responders of Canada’s energy infrastructure. As a TEER 2 occupation, these workers construct, maintain, and repair overhead and underground electrical power transmission and distribution lines. In 2026, as Canada faces increasingly volatile weather patterns and a massive push for "Grid Resilience," these workers are classified as Critical Emergency Talent. For immigration, this role is a premier candidate for Express Entry Category-Based Selection and the April 2026 TR to PR Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level2Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group722 – Electrical trades and electrical power line and cable workersMinor Group7220 – Electricians (except industrial and power system)NOC Code72203

duties: Installation: Install, maintain, and repair electrical auxiliary equipment such as transformers, lightning arresters, switches, and insulators.
Pole & Tower Work: Erect and maintain steel, wood, or concrete poles and towers; climb ladders or use bucket trucks to reach high-voltage lines.
Stringing: String, splice, and terminate conductors (wires) between poles and towers using specialized winches and tensioning equipment.
Underground Operations: Install and repair underground cables and related equipment in trenches and manholes.
Troubleshooting: Locate and repair faults in lines and equipment during power outages or system failures.
Safety & Compliance: Ensure strict adherence to high-voltage safety protocols and environmental regulations; conduct routine patrols to identify potential hazards.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a 3-to-4-year apprenticeship program for powerline technicians is the standard route.
Work Experience: Alternatively, a combination of over 4 years of work experience and some industry-certified courses in electricity may be accepted.

Trade Certification (Mandatory/Voluntary): * Trade certification is compulsory in Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island, New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, Saskatchewan, Alberta, British Columbia, and the Yukon.It is available, but voluntary, in Manitoba and the other territories.
It is available, but voluntary, in Manitoba and the other territories.
Red Seal Endorsement: Highly recommended for all immigrants to ensure national recognition and maximum points for PR through the Federal Skilled Trades Program.
Physical fitness for climbing, working at heights, and a valid heavy-vehicle driver's license (Class 3 or 1) are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, experienced Powerline Technicians in major utilities often earn annual salaries between $105,000 and $145,000 inclusive of overtime, storm-restoration bonuses, and travel allowances.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the utilities sector will face a significant wave of retirements over the next decade. As Canada integrates millions of new Electric Vehicles (EVs) and smart-grid technologies, the demand for technicians capable of upgrading neighborhood distribution networks ensures long-term job security through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Powerline Technicians in 2026 is Very Good to High. In Ontario, British Columbia, and Alberta, a combination of aging grid infrastructure and the rapid expansion of renewable energy sites (wind and solar farms) has created a significant labor deficit. According to the 2026 Electricity Human Resources Canada (EHRC) report, the industry is seeing a double-digit vacancy rate in rural and northern regions, making it an ideal time for experienced international lineworkers to secure LMIA-supported job offers.
Essential Service Status: Enjoy unparalleled job security; lineworkers are the first called during storm restorations and infrastructure upgrades.
Top-Tier Compensation: This trade consistently ranks as one of the highest-paying manual trades in Canada, with substantial overtime opportunities and emergency standby premiums.
High-Tech Fieldwork: Utilize advanced hydraulic buckets, specialized off-road tensioning equipment, and drone technology for line inspections.
Strategic PR Pathway: This role is a priority in Express Entry Trade draws, often requiring lower CRS scores for an Invitation to Apply (ITA).
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Highly targeted under the Trade Occupations category in 2026.
New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A temporary measure targeting 33,000 skilled workers already in Canada, specifically focusing on essential resource and utility sectors.
BC PNP Skilled Worker: Priority for lineworkers supporting the province’s massive hydroelectric and renewable energy expansion.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Ideal for those currently working in the province's energy and power distribution sectors.
